description: Commercial Brokerage Placement Policies — rules for carrier selection, coverage adequacy, premium benchmarking, financial strength gating, placement optimisation, and quote completeness
regulatory_framework: AM Best Rating Standards, ISO Commercial Lines

policies.name: Property TIV Carrier Routing
policies.description: Route property submissions to appropriate carriers based on Total Insured Value and hazard profile

criteria.action: Standard market carriers (e.g., Travelers, Intact, Aviva). Proceed with direct submission.
criteria.rationale: Small-to-mid commercial property within standard carrier appetite thresholds.

criteria.action: Mid-market and specialty carriers; consider surplus lines. Review available markets before submission.
criteria.rationale: Above standard threshold or poor protection class limits available standard markets.

criteria.condition: TIV > $25M OR coastal property OR flood zone A/AE
criteria.action: Wholesale broker / MGA referral; Lloyd's markets. Risk exceeds retail carrier appetite.
criteria.rationale: Large or high-hazard risk requires specialty market access through wholesale channel.

policies.name: Occupancy Class Eligibility
policies.description: Determine carrier eligibility based on occupancy type and associated hazard grade

criteria.action: Standard market eligible. Submit to preferred carrier panel.
criteria.rationale: Low-to-moderate hazard occupancies are within most standard carrier appetites.

criteria.action: Specialty market required. Verify carrier appetite for specific occupancy before submission.
criteria.rationale: Higher hazard occupancies have limited standard market availability.

policies.name: Building Limit Adequacy
policies.description: Validate that the quoted building limit covers the replacement cost value to avoid co-insurance penalties

criteria.action: Coverage is adequate. Client is fully covered for building replacement.
criteria.rationale: Building limit meets or exceeds replacement cost — no co-insurance exposure.

criteria.action: Co-insurance penalty risk. Recommend increasing building limit to full replacement cost value.
criteria.rationale: Below 100% but above 80% co-insurance threshold — partial claims may still be paid in full but risk exists.

criteria.action: Significant co-insurance exposure. Carrier will reject or reduce claim proportionally. Escalate to broker immediately.
criteria.rationale: Below 80% co-insurance threshold triggers penalty clauses — client bears proportional share of any loss.

policies.calculation: Co-insurance penalty = (Carried limit / Required limit) * Loss amount

policies.name: Business Interruption Limit Check
policies.description: Validate that business interruption coverage is sufficient relative to reported revenues

criteria.action: BI coverage is adequate for a full-year interruption scenario.
criteria.rationale: 12-month coverage provides sufficient runway for most recovery scenarios.

criteria.action: BI coverage may be insufficient for extended loss events. Recommend reviewing with client.
criteria.rationale: Sub-12-month coverage leaves client exposed if recovery exceeds policy period.

references: ISO Business Income Coverage Form CP 00 30

policies.name: Premium Market Comparison
policies.description: Rate the quoted premium against the market median for this risk profile

criteria.action: Below Market — verify coverage terms are equivalent. Low premium may indicate narrower coverage or carrier pricing error.
criteria.rationale: Significantly below market may indicate reduced coverage, higher deductibles, or exclusions not present in other quotes.

criteria.action: Market Rate — premium is within acceptable range. No action required.
criteria.rationale: Within ±15% of market median is considered competitive.

criteria.action: Above Market — negotiate with carrier or seek additional quotes from competing markets.
criteria.rationale: Premium exceeds market range — may warrant pushback or alternative carrier submission.

references: Broker market intelligence and historical quote data

policies.name: Rate per $100 of TIV Benchmark
policies.description: Compare the implied rate per $100 of TIV against class benchmarks

criteria.action: Rate is at or below class benchmark. Competitive pricing confirmed.
criteria.rationale: Rate per $100 of TIV is within expected range for the occupancy and construction class.

criteria.action: Rate exceeds benchmark by more than 25%. Investigate loss history, construction quality, or carrier surcharges.
criteria.rationale: Elevated rate suggests carrier is pricing in additional risk factors not reflected in standard class rates.

policies.name: Carrier Financial Strength Gate
policies.description: Evaluate carrier solvency and financial health from AM Best profile before including in placement recommendation

criteria.condition: FinancialStrengthRating IN ['A++', 'A+', 'A'] AND RatingOutlook != 'Under Review - Negative'
criteria.action: Eligible for placement. Display FSR badge in green on quote comparison.
criteria.rationale: AM Best A or better indicates superior financial stability and strong claims-paying ability.

criteria.action: Eligible for placement with note. Display FSR badge in yellow; add monitoring note to broker.
criteria.rationale: A- is acceptable but warrants ongoing monitoring of carrier financial performance.

criteria.condition: FinancialStrengthRating IN ['B++', 'B+'] OR RatingOutlook IN ['Negative', 'Under Review - Negative']
criteria.action: Flag for broker review. Include financial strength warning prominently in quote comparison.
criteria.rationale: B-range carriers carry materially higher insolvency risk and may not meet client or E&O requirements.

criteria.condition: FinancialStrengthRating NOT IN ['A++','A+','A','A-','B++','B+'] OR FinancialStrengthRating IS NULL
criteria.action: Exclude from automated placement recommendation. Broker must manually override with documented justification.
criteria.rationale: Unrated or below B+ carriers pose unacceptable financial risk for client protection.

references: AM Best Financial Strength Rating Guide

policies.name: Carrier Combined Ratio Check
policies.description: Assess carrier profitability via combined ratio as a secondary financial health indicator

criteria.action: Carrier is operating profitably. No additional financial concern.
criteria.rationale: Combined ratio below 100% indicates underwriting profit — carrier is financially healthy.

criteria.action: Carrier operating near breakeven. Monitor for premium increases or coverage restrictions at renewal.
criteria.rationale: Marginal underwriting losses may lead to market corrections affecting renewal terms.

criteria.action: Carrier sustaining significant underwriting losses. Factor into placement risk assessment.
criteria.rationale: Persistently high combined ratios signal potential solvency stress or aggressive market exit.

policies.calculation: Combined Ratio = (Incurred Losses + Expenses) / Earned Premiums

policies.name: Best Value Placement Score
policies.description: Composite scoring model for ranking carrier quotes and generating placement recommendations

scoring_factors.description: Lower premium scores higher, adjusted for coverage breadth
Fewer exclusions and higher sublimits score higher
FSR tier scoring with combined ratio and NWP/surplus adjustments
All required fields populated without ambiguity

policies.scoring_methodology: Each factor is scored 0–100 and weighted. Total placement score = Σ(factor_score × weight). Quotes with total score >= 75 are recommended; 50–74 are acceptable; below 50 require broker justification.

policies.name: Layered Program Structure Guidance
policies.description: Guidelines for structuring multi-carrier layered programs on large risks

criteria.action: Consider layered program structure with primary and excess layers to achieve full coverage.
criteria.rationale: Single-carrier capacity constraints on large risks require multi-carrier program design.

criteria.action: Lead carrier share below 25% may reduce their commitment to claims handling. Negotiate higher lead share.
criteria.rationale: Lead carrier engagement and claims responsiveness correlates with their share of the program.

references: Lloyd's market minimum lead line guidance

policies.name: Required Quote Fields Check
policies.description: Validate that carrier quotes contain all required comparison fields before inclusion in placement analysis

criteria.condition: all required_fields are present AND non-null
criteria.action: Quote is complete for comparison. Include in placement analysis.
criteria.rationale: All mandatory data points are present for accurate quote-to-quote comparison.

criteria.action: Quote is incomplete. Request missing fields from carrier before including in comparison.
criteria.rationale: Incomplete quotes cannot be fairly compared and may mislead placement decisions.

policies.name: Quote Terms Consistency Check
policies.description: Ensure quoted terms are consistent and comparable across carriers

criteria.condition: all quotes use same valuation_basis (replacement_cost OR actual_cash_value)
criteria.action: Valuation basis is consistent across quotes. Comparison is valid.
criteria.rationale: Apples-to-apples comparison requires consistent valuation methodology.

criteria.action: Normalize quotes to same valuation basis before comparison. Flag difference to broker.
criteria.rationale: Replacement cost vs. actual cash value can produce materially different claim outcomes.
